provenance: Extracted mechanically from corpus/dumps (gh_dump over the official Wasp example corpus, github.com/ar0551/Wasp @ corpus/wasp-upstream). Every entry lists its source file(s).

rule_text.component: Wasp_Rule From Text (input TXT, one rule per line/panel item)
rule_text.form: PART|CONN_PART|CONN  (pipe between part name and connection index, underscore between the two halves)

connection_type_grammar.component: Wasp_Rules Generator (input GR); connection type names come from the T input of Connection From Plane/Direction
connection_type_grammar.form: TYPE>TYPE  (each line permits pairing any two connections carrying these type strings)

graph_grammar.component: Wasp_Graph-Grammar Aggregation (input RULES, panel text directly - no Rule From Text)
graph_grammar.form: PART|CONN_PART|CONN>node_node  (a rule_text rule followed by '>' and the graph-node rewrite labels)

rule_syntax.direction_note: Rules are directional (scribble in 0_01: 'NB: Rules are directional! So if you want to connect 1 to 2 and 2 to 1, you need to write two separate rules.')
rule_syntax.evidence: grammars[] below, all verbatim from corpus panels

basic_aggregation.description: Single part, connections from direction, Rule components merged into Stochastic Aggregation; geometry extracted via Get Part Geometry

multipart_aggregation.description: Multiple Basic Parts (Merge'd) into one Stochastic Aggregation
chain: Wasp_Filter Parts by Name[NameFilter].PART_OUT -> Wasp_Get Part Geometry.PART

vertex_edge_connections.description: Connection From Plane fed by planes constructed on vertices/edges

part_geometry_replacement.description: Deconstruct Part TR output used to transform replacement geometry
chain: Wasp_Filter Parts by Name[NameFilter].MASK -> Cull Pattern.Cull Pattern

transform_start_part.description: Transform Part to relocate the aggregation seed part

part_attributes.description: Attribute components attached to Basic Part ATTR input
chain: Wasp_Get Attribute by Name[GetAttr].VAL -> Custom Preview.Geometry
Wasp_Get Attribute by Name[GetAttr].VAL -> Custom Preview.Material

save_load_aggregation.description: Save Aggregation to File / Load Aggregation from File round-trip
chain: Panel[FILE TO IMPORT].Panel -> Wasp_Load Aggregation from File.FILE
Wasp_Stochastic Aggregation[StochasticAggregation].AGGR -> Wasp_Save Aggregation to File.AGGR
Button[CLICK TO SAVE!!!].Button -> Wasp_Save Aggregation to File.SAVE
Wasp_Load Aggregation from File[LoadAggr].PART_OUT -> Wasp_Get Part Geometry.PART

fixed_seed.description: Stochastic Aggregation with SEED slider for reproducibility

parts_catalog.description: Parts Catalog limiting per-part instance counts
chain: Wasp_Filter Parts by Name[NameFilter].PART_OUT -> Wasp_Get Part Geometry.PART

rules_generator.description: Rules Generator producing all/self/grammar-based rule sets
chain: Wasp_Filter Parts by Name[NameFilter].PART_OUT -> Wasp_Get Part Geometry.PART

rules_connection_types.description: Connection type strings (T input) driving Rules Generator compatibility

rule_grammar_text.description: Connection-type grammar (TYPE>TYPE panel into Rules Generator GR; type strings assigned via Connection From Direction T input)
chain: Wasp_Filter Parts by Name[NameFilter].PART_OUT -> Wasp_Get Part Geometry.PART

rules_visualizer.description: Rules Visualizer previewing rule pairings on parts
chain: Wasp_Filter Parts by Name[NameFilter].PART_OUT -> Wasp_Get Part Geometry.PART
Wasp_Rules Visualizer[RuleViz].BP -> Wasp_Filter Parts by Name.PART
Wasp_Rules Visualizer[RuleViz].NP -> Wasp_Filter Parts by Name.PART

rules_from_aggregation.description: Extract rules back out of an existing aggregation
chain: Wasp_Get Attribute by Name[GetAttr].VAL -> Custom Preview.Geometry

field_driven_aggregation.description: Field (from points + values) driving Field-driven Aggregation

templates._provenance: Machine-parseable stage templates for the v0.5 expander (same wire syntax as arch_patterns.json: 'slot:<name> -> <ref>.<Param>' / '<ref>.<Out> -> <ref>.<In>'). Wasp components are placed via the local registry ('wasp': <registry key>), never by stock-component name/guid; param names are the exact recorded names from the corpus chains and param_aliases (PART/RULES/N/SEED/RESET, outputs AGGR then PART_OUT).

wasp.stochastic_aggregation.explainer:
Grows the structure: places N copies of the part(s) one by one, each placement chosen randomly among rule-legal, collision-free options.
Same seed = same growth; change the seed for a different but equally legal result.

parts.note: PART output(s) of Wasp Basic/Advanced Part (create_wasp_part part_id)

rules.note: R output of Rule From Text / Rules Generator (define_rules rules_component_id)

seed.note: 0_08 feeds SEED from a Series sweep (no slider range evidence); the expander derives the slider range from the bound value

components.note: corpus RESET feeders: Button (0_01) / Boolean Toggle (0_08); the toggle is the bridge-settable one (set_toggle). Pulse it (reset_aggregation macro) before reading PART_OUT.

wasp.stochastic_aggregation.notes: Wire evidence (corpus chains): Wasp_Basic Part.PART -> .PART, Number Slider -> .N, rules .R -> .RULES, Series -> .SEED, Button/Boolean Toggle -> .RESET, .PART_OUT -> Wasp_Get Part Geometry.PART (PART_OUT is what extraction reads; AGGR only feeds graph/save components). run_aggregation remains the full-service macro (auto RESET pulse); this template is the composable stage form.
